methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Magdrive, a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mark Stokes and Thomas Clayson, has raised $22.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises five public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though direct comparables at identical stage and valuation tier are sparse.

Stage-progression analysis provides the primary quantitative anchor. Using total raised ($22.5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $56M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace filter (fewer than three datapoints survived screening). Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed because Magdrive's revenue is not disclosed, precluding P/S multiple application despite a sector median of 64.2x across the five public tickers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $48M to $65M, with $56M as the point estimate, derived solely from stage-progression methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data (limiting multiple-based approaches), the thinness of private seed-stage comparables in the sector, and whether the 2.5x step-up multiplier reflects Magdrive's specific risk profile and market position. This single-method reliance underscores the need for additional data on peer funding rounds and company-specific milestones to narrow the range.
